# checks.tests.TestsCheck { #pycmdcheck.checks.tests.TestsCheck }

```python
checks.tests.TestsCheck()
```

Run package tests and report results.

Executes the package's test suite using the configured test runner
(pytest by default) and reports pass/fail status. Supports pytest
and unittest runners.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The check identifier ("tests").

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description of this check.

## Configuration Options {.doc-section .doc-section-configuration-options}

runner (str): Test runner to use ("pytest" or "unittest").
    Defaults to "pytest".
args (list[str]): Additional arguments to pass to the test runner.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Run tests with default settings (pytest):

```python
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> check = TestsCheck()
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), {"enabled": True})
>>> result.name
'tests'
```

Configure to use unittest:

```python
>>> config = {"enabled": True, "runner": "unittest"}
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), config)
```

Pass additional pytest arguments:

```python
>>> config = {"enabled": True, "args": ["-x", "--cov"]}
>>> result = check.run(Path("."), config)
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.tests.TestsCheck.run) | Run tests and return results. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.tests.TestsCheck.run }

```python
checks.tests.TestsCheck.run(package_path, config)
```

Run tests and return results.

Executes the test suite in the package's tests/ or test/ directory.
The test runner is determined by the ``runner`` config option.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory containing tests.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary with options:  - runner (str): "pytest" or "unittest" (default: "pytest") - args (list[str]): Additional arguments for the runner

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult with:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - OK status if all tests pass

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - WARNING status if no tests found

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - ERROR status if tests fail or runner not installed

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - SKIPPED status if the test runner is not installed
